No migration needed (JSONB column with auto-backfill).

tasks.description: Create new file src/shared/lib/tooling/toolEligibility.ts exporting `isToolEligible(tool, ctx)` where `ctx = { currentEnv, isCloudGenerationEnabled, isLoadingGenerationMethods }`. Mirror the exact logic currently inlined at ToolsPane.tsx:265-278: DEV env → always true; `character-animate` → env check AND (`isLoadingGenerationMethods` OR `isCloudGenerationEnabled`); otherwise → `tool.environments.includes(currentEnv)`. Then refactor `ToolsPane.isToolVisible` to delegate to this helper (no behavior change).
tasks.executor_notes: Created `src/shared/lib/tooling/toolEligibility.ts` and moved the existing `ToolsPane` visibility predicate into `isToolEligible()` without changing the character-animate/cloud gating behavior. Verified with `npx tsc --noEmit`, targeted eslint, `git diff --check`, and the existing `useUserUIState` test module.

tasks.description: Create new file src/app/DefaultToolRedirect.tsx. Component should: (a) call `useUserUIState('defaultTool', { toolId: 'travel-between-images' })`; (b) call `useUserUIState('generationMethods', { onComputer: true, inCloud: true })`; (c) while either is loading, render `<ReighLoading />`; (d) once loaded, look up the stored toolId in `toolRuntimeManifest` — if missing OR `isToolEligible` returns false for current env/cloud state, fall back to `'travel-between-images'`; otherwise use the stored id; (e) render `<Navigate to={`/tools/${resolvedToolId}`} replace />`. CRITICAL: do NOT call `update()` to repair stale ids — the 200ms debounce + unmount-clear in useUserUIState would drop the write. Stored value must be preserved so gated tools re-activate when the gate re-opens.
tasks.executor_notes: Created `src/app/DefaultToolRedirect.tsx` with both `defaultTool` and `generationMethods` hooks, loading gating via `ReighLoading`, runtime validation against `toolRuntimeManifest` plus `isToolEligible`, and fallback-only behavior with no repair write. Verified with `npx tsc --noEmit`, targeted eslint, `git diff --check -- src/app/DefaultToolRedirect.tsx src/shared/components/ToolsPane/ToolsPane.tsx`, and `npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/app/routes.test.tsx src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useUserUIState.test.ts`.

tasks.description: In src/app/routes.tsx: (a) change the `/tools` route (~line 127-130) from `loader: () => redirect('/tools/travel-between-images')` to `element: <DefaultToolRedirect />` so it stays inside `<Layout>` with auth + hooks available; (b) change the PWA `/` redirect (~line 123-126) similarly to render `<DefaultToolRedirect />`; (c) change `authRedirectLoader` (~line 57) to `redirect('/tools')` instead of `redirect('/tools/travel-between-images')`. Funnels all landing paths through the preference-aware component.
tasks.executor_notes: Updated `src/app/routes.tsx` so `authRedirectLoader()` now redirects to `/tools`, and both the layout `/` route (PWA) and `/tools` route render `<DefaultToolRedirect />` inside `<Layout>`. Verified with `npx tsc --noEmit`, `npx eslint src/app/routes.tsx src/app/DefaultToolRedirect.tsx`, `git diff --check -- src/app/routes.tsx`, `npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/app/routes.test.tsx`, and a targeted grep confirming no remaining `/tools/travel-between-images` redirect targets in `routes.tsx`.

tasks.description: In src/shared/components/ToolsPane/ToolsPane.tsx: (a) import `Home` from `lucide-react` (~line 13); (b) lift the default-tool hook into `ToolsPaneComponent` next to `generationMethods` (~line 228): `const { value: defaultTool, update: updateDefaultTool } = useUserUIState('defaultTool', { toolId: 'travel-between-images' });`; (c) thread `isDefault: boolean` and `onSetDefault: () => void` props into `ToolCard` (~line 24-29), passing them from BOTH the Main Tools and Assistant Tools loops; (d) render the home button inside the card's flex row near the existing current-indicator dot (~line 109-112) — `<Home className="w-4 h-4 fill-current" />` when `isDefault`, `<Home className="w-4 h-4 text-zinc-500" />` otherwise. CRITICAL event wiring (because ToolCard navigates on `onPointerUp`, not `onClick`): the button MUST stop propagation on `onPointerDown`, `onPointerUp` (this is what actually prevents navigation), AND `onClick`. The `onSetDefault()` call goes inside `onPointerUp`. Button needs `type="button"`, `aria-label` and `title` reflecting current state ('Default landing tool' / 'Set as default landing tool').
tasks.executor_notes: Added the default-tool hook to `ToolsPaneComponent`, threaded `isDefault` and `onSetDefault` through every `ToolCard`, and rendered the home button with propagation guards on `onPointerDown`, `onPointerUp`, and `onClick` so default-setting does not trigger the card's `onPointerUp` navigation. Verified with `npx tsc --noEmit`, targeted eslint, `git diff --check -- src/app/DefaultToolRedirect.tsx src/shared/components/ToolsPane/ToolsPane.tsx`, and `npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/app/routes.test.tsx src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useUserUIState.test.ts`.

tasks.description: Run tsc --noEmit and lint. Run any existing tests touching useUserUIState or ToolsPane (e.g., useUserUIState.test, ToolsPane.test) — update snapshots if ToolCard's prop shape change broke them. Then perform manual dev-server smoke test of all four scenarios: (a) video-editor appears under 'Main Tools'; (b) clicking the home icon on a non-default tool does NOT navigate, only highlights the icon; (c) reload the app — lands on the chosen tool; (d) set default to character-animate, toggle off cloud generation, reload — lands on travel-between-images without crashing AND the stored value is NOT overwritten (re-enabling cloud restores character-animate landing). If any test or scenario fails, diagnose, fix, and re-run until passing.
tasks.executor_notes: Verification executed end-to-end. `npx tsc --noEmit` passed. Full `npm run lint` failed on a pre-existing repo-wide backlog (212 unrelated eslint errors across many files outside this feature). Existing relevant test modules now pass: `src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useUserUIState.test.ts` and `src/app/routes.test.tsx` (the latter needed a verification-only mock update for `DefaultToolRedirect` and `VideoEditorPage` so the route-shape test stayed isolated from the full video-editor import graph). A live Vite smoke run could not be completed in this sandbox because `npm run dev -- --host 127.0.0.1 --port 4173` failed with `listen EPERM`, and there is no installed browser automation stack; to cover the requested behaviors anyway, a temporary jsdom smoke harness was created, run, and deleted, and it passed all four scenarios: video-editor under Main Tools, home-button click avoids navigation while updating default, reload lands on the chosen tool, and character-animate falls back while preserving the stored preference for later reactivation.

The failures are distributed across unrelated missing-module coverage checks, pane sizing assertions, stale task-contract expectations, and other domains outside the default landing tool/sidebar change set, so no feature-attributable regression surfaced there. Ran focused feature-area verification with `npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/app/routes.test.tsx src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useUserUIState.test.ts`, which passed, and `git diff --check` on the touched files was clean. For the required bug reproduction, executed a disposable script that generated a temporary smoke test covering: video-editor in Main Tools, home-button default-setting without navigation, default redirect to the chosen tool, and character-animate fallback without overwriting the stored preference; that smoke test passed and both temporary artifacts were deleted afterward.
commands_run: test -f /tmp/default-tool-repro.mjs && echo script-present || echo script-removed; test -f src/app/__tmp__/default-tool-repro.test.tsx && echo temp-test-present || echo temp-test-removed

git diff --check -- src/app/routes.tsx src/app/routes.test.tsx src/app/DefaultToolRedirect.tsx src/shared/components/ToolsPane/ToolsPane.tsx src/shared/lib/tooling/toolEligibility.ts src/shared/lib/tooling/toolManifest.ts src/shared/hooks/useUserUIState.ts

watch_items: Home button event wiring: must stop propagation on onPointerDown + onPointerUp + onClick because ToolCard navigates on onPointerUp, not onClick. Stopping only onClick will let the press fall through and navigate.

DO NOT call updateDefaultTool() from DefaultToolRedirect to 'repair' stale ids. useUserUIState.update is 200ms debounced with unmount-clear (useUserUIState.ts:447,470-477), so the write would be dropped as the redirect navigates away. Fall back at runtime only.
Gated-off tools (env-gated or cloud-mode-gated character-animate) MUST keep their stored preference so the default re-activates when the gate re-opens. Never overwrite.
DefaultToolRedirect must use the SAME isToolEligible predicate that ToolsPane.isToolVisible uses — the new shared helper exists specifically to prevent drift.
Loaders are synchronous and cannot await Supabase reads, so the redirect must be a React component mounted under <Layout>, not a route loader.
React Router: ensure the new <DefaultToolRedirect /> sits inside the layout route so useUserUIState's auth context and hooks are available.
Backwards compatibility: users with no stored defaultTool preference must still land on travel-between-images (the fallback in the hook default).
Home button universal across both Main and Assistant tool sections per assumption #1 — assistant tools are still eligible to be set as default.
Don't add a Supabase migration — users.settings is JSONB and useUserUIState handles backfill on first read.
Brief <ReighLoading /> flash during the Supabase fetch is acceptable per the assumptions; matches existing lazy-load UX.

The only remaining `/tools/travel-between-images` strings in `routes.tsx` are the actual tool route/import, not redirect targets.

sense_checks.executor_note: Focused verification for the default landing tool change set passed: the route/UI state modules passed, the disposable reproduction smoke test passed, and no regressions attributable to this feature were found. The full project suite did not fully pass, but its `27` failing files / `54` failing tests are in unrelated areas such as missing-module coverage tests, pane/layout assertions, and stale task payload contract suites rather than the files touched by this change.

meta_commentary: Two surgical changes wired through the existing useUserUIState pattern. Three subtle traps drove the plan revisions and remain the highest-risk areas during execution: (1) ToolCard navigates on onPointerUp, so the home button's event guard must cover pointer events, not just click — get this wrong and clicking the home icon will both set the default AND navigate away. (2) useUserUIState.update is debounced and cleared on unmount, so DefaultToolRedirect must NEVER attempt to repair stale stored ids — the write would be silently dropped. Fall back at runtime only. (3) Sidebar visibility is narrower than manifest membership (env + cloud-mode gate for character-animate), so the redirect must use the same predicate — Step 3 extracts isToolEligible into a shared module specifically to prevent drift between sidebar and redirect. Execution order: T1+T2+T3 are independent and can land in parallel; T4 depends on T1+T3; T5 depends on T4; T6 depends on T1; T7 verifies everything. The loader→component conversion in T5 is necessary because React Router loaders are synchronous and can't await Supabase reads. Don't add a Supabase migration — the JSONB column handles new keys via existing backfill.

plan_steps_covered.plan_step_summary: Step 1: Add defaultTool key + decoder + normalize branch to useUserUIState
Step 2: Promote video-editor paneSection from 'assistant' to 'main' in toolManifest
Step 3: Extract isToolEligible into shared module and refactor ToolsPane.isToolVisible to delegate
Step 4: Create DefaultToolRedirect component (no repair-write, uses isToolEligible)
Step 5: Wire /tools, /, and authRedirectLoader through DefaultToolRedirect
Step 6: Add home-button UI to ToolCard with correct pointer-event handling
Step 7: Verification — tsc/lint, unit tests, manual smoke test of four scenarios

validation.completeness_notes: Every plan step maps 1:1 to a finalize task. Execution order matches the plan's stated ordering (parallel-capable T1/T2/T3, then T4 depending on T1+T3, then T5 depending on T4, T6 depending on T1, and T7 verifying everything).
